mechanism_narrative: Declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals weakening labor demand and future earnings contraction. Equity investors reprice risk downward in anticipation of reduced corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130738.0 thousand as of March 2011
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with fragile labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months where PAYEMS declines month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remains near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%), indicating an extended monetary accommodation regime. This persistently low rate environment sustains equity valuations through compressed discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 1% or t-test p > 0.10) when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with expanding housing starts (HOUST) signals strengthening consumer demand and construction activity. This dual demand expansion drives corporate revenue growth expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 389320.0 million and HOUST at 479.0 thousand as of February-March 2011
regime_hint: early-cycle expansion with synchronized demand recovery
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher when both RSAFS and HOUST are rising simultaneously (mean return differential < 0.5% or t-test p > 0.15), or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month, signaling contraction in manufacturing output and capacity utilization. This supply-side weakness constrains earnings growth and signals deteriorating business conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 93.5904 index as of March 2011
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where INDPRO declines > 0.5% MoM, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 8.5%, indicating labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power. Higher unemployment triggers downward revisions to earnings forecasts and equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative when UNRATE > 8.5% (mean return > 0% or t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 0.5%, creating negative real rates. This monetary accommodation in an inflationary environment erodes equity real returns and signals future rate hikes, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 223.49 index and FEDFUNDS at 0.14% as of March 2011
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower when both conditions are met versus baseline (mean return differential > -0.3% or t-test p > 0.15), or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly shows deceleration below 2% annualized rate, signaling weakening aggregate demand and economic momentum. Investors reduce equity allocations in anticipation of lower corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly annualized growth rate falls below 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13380.7 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q4 2010
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative when GDPC1 growth < 2.0% (mean return > 0.5% or t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate above 500 thousand units annualized while retail sales (RSAFS) remain stable or rising, signaling construction-led demand recovery. This housing-driven expansion increases multiplier effects through employment and consumer confidence, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above 500 thousand units annualized
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 479.0 thousand and RSAFS at 389320.0 million as of February-March 2011
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are not significantly higher when HOUST > 500k and RSAFS is stable/rising (mean return differential < 0.2% or t-test p > 0.20), or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant at p < 0.10.
